description: A single node in the pipeline has failed with an explicit error in the logs. The root cause is visible and directly stated.

node_cluster_manager.message: Starting scheduled capacity check for production cluster
High resource reservation detected - services requesting 95% of cluster capacity
Unable to schedule new service instance - insufficient resources
Resource allocation mismatch detected - reserved > actual usage by 340%
Misconfiguration detected in resource management - services reserving excessive CPU/RAM

node_monitoring.message: CPU allocation ratio: 87%, Memory allocation ratio: 92%

node_autoscaler.message: Scaling event blocked due to resource constraints

node_deployer.message: New instance deployment failed - no available capacity despite 45% physical resources free

node_alert.message: Service degradation detected - multiple deployment failures

node_ops.message: Resource quota configuration error confirmed - services over-reserving resources
Applying resource quota fix to cluster configuration

ground_truth.root_cause_description: Misconfiguration in cluster resource management caused services to reserve excessive resources (CPU/RAM) they didn't use, preventing new instances from starting despite available physical capacity.

hints: Look at nodes with CRITICAL log entries first
Check which node has the earliest ERROR-level log
The root cause node has FAILED status and explicit CRITICAL message
